walkthrough.title: Unfold the Game Board
walkthrough.text: Let us get Blood Rage set up. First, unfold the Yggdrasil game board and place it in the center of the table where everyone can reach it. The board shows eight outer provinces arranged in a ring around a central area called Yggdrasil. Each province has village spaces where you will place your Viking figures during the game.

walkthrough.title: Set Up Valhalla
walkthrough.text: Find the Valhalla sheet and place it beside the board. This is the holding area for destroyed figures. When your Vikings fall in battle or get caught in Ragnarok, they go here. Do not worry though, death is temporary in Blood Rage. Your figures come back at the end of each Age.

walkthrough.title: Prepare the Ragnarok Tokens
walkthrough.text: Shuffle the three Ragnarok tokens face down. Reveal one token and place the Doom token on the matching province. That province will be destroyed at the end of Age one. Set the other two tokens face down nearby for Ages two and three.

walkthrough.title: Place the Pillage Tokens
walkthrough.text: Place one Pillage token on each outer province with the reward side face down. These tokens represent the spoils of war. When you successfully pillage a province, you flip its token to reveal a reward, usually a stat upgrade for your clan.

walkthrough.title: Adjust for Player Count
walkthrough.text: If you are playing with four players, use all eight outer provinces. With three players, close one province by removing it from play. With two players, close three provinces. Fewer players means a tighter board, which keeps the action intense.

walkthrough.title: Set Up the Age Track
walkthrough.text: Place the Saga token on the first position of the Age track. The game spans three Ages, and the Saga token tracks your progress through them. Also sort the cards into three separate decks by their Age number on the back. Set them near the board.

walkthrough.title: Choose Your Clan
walkthrough.text: Each player picks a clan and takes all of its components: one Leader miniature, eight Warrior miniatures, one Ship miniature, a clan sheet, and the matching clan tokens and plastic bases. The clans are mechanically identical at the start, so pick whichever Viking crew speaks to you.

walkthrough.title: Set Up Your Clan Sheet
walkthrough.text: Place your clan sheet in front of you. Set your three stat tracks to their starting values. Rage starts at six, which is how many action points you get each Age. Axes starts at three, which is how much glory you earn per battle won. Horns starts at four, which is the maximum number of figures you can have on the board at once.

walkthrough.title: Place Glory Markers
walkthrough.text: Each player places their two plastic Glory tracking markers on position zero of the glory track around the board. These track your victory points throughout the game. You will move them up as you win battles, complete quests, and earn rewards.

walkthrough.title: Prepare Your Reserve
walkthrough.text: All of your figures start in your reserve, which is the area beside your clan sheet. At the beginning of each Age, every figure returns here. You will place them onto the board during the Action phase using the Invade action.

walkthrough.title: Set Out the Monsters
walkthrough.text: Place the four Monster miniatures near the board where everyone can see them. These are the Troll, Sea Serpent, Fire Giant, and Mystic. Monsters are not owned by anyone yet. You recruit them during the game by drafting and playing Monster upgrade cards.

walkthrough.title: Select First Player
walkthrough.text: Choose a random player to go first. Play proceeds clockwise from the first player. You are now ready to begin the first Age of Blood Rage. Let us learn the rules.

walkthrough.title: The Three Ages
walkthrough.text: Blood Rage is played over three Ages, and each Age follows the same six phases. Think of each Age as a complete round of play. Cards get more powerful with each passing Age, so the game builds to an explosive finale in Age three. At the start of each Age, your Rage refills and all your destroyed figures return from Valhalla to your reserve.

walkthrough.title: Phase One: Gods' Gifts
walkthrough.text: Each Age begins with a card draft. Deal eight cards from the current Age deck to each player. Look at your hand, pick one card to keep, and pass the rest clockwise. Keep drafting one card at a time until you have picked six cards total. The two remaining cards from each hand are discarded without being seen. This draft determines your entire strategy for the Age, so choose wisely.

walkthrough.title: What You Are Drafting
walkthrough.text: There are three types of cards in each Age deck. Battle cards have an Axes icon and give you strength bonuses in combat. Upgrade cards have a Horns icon and grant permanent abilities when played to your clan sheet. Quest cards have a Helmet icon and award glory if you meet their conditions by the end of the Age. A good hand usually includes a mix of all three types.

walkthrough.title: Phase Two: The Action Phase
walkthrough.text: This is the heart of the game. Players take turns clockwise, performing one action per turn by spending Rage. Your Rage is your action point budget for the entire Age. When you run out of Rage or decide to stop, you pass. Once you pass, you cannot take any more actions this Age. The phase ends when all players have passed.

walkthrough.title: Action: Invade
walkthrough.text: To invade, take a figure from your reserve and place it on any empty village space in an outer province. Warriors cost one Rage to invade. Your Leader costs zero Rage, making it a free deployment. Monsters cost Rage equal to their strength value. You can also invade with your Ship by placing it on a fjord between two provinces. Remember, your total figures on the board cannot exceed your Horns limit.

walkthrough.title: Action: March
walkthrough.text: Marching costs two Rage and lets you move any number of your figures from one province to other provinces. Your figures can split up and go to different destinations. Ships march along fjords. This is how you reposition your forces for upcoming battles or flee the doomed Ragnarok province.

walkthrough.title: Action: Upgrade
walkthrough.text: Playing an Upgrade card from your hand costs zero Rage. Place the card on your clan sheet in the matching slot to gain its permanent ability. Upgrades include Warrior upgrades that boost strength, Leader upgrades that enhance your Leader, Ship upgrades, and Monster recruitment cards. When you play a Monster upgrade, take the matching Monster miniature and add it to your clan. You can now invade with that Monster.

walkthrough.title: Action: Quest
walkthrough.text: Committing a Quest card also costs zero Rage. Take a Quest card from your hand and place it face down beside your clan sheet. It stays hidden until the Quest phase at the end of the Age. If you meet the quest condition at that point, you score the glory reward. If you fail, the card is simply discarded with no penalty.

walkthrough.title: Action: Pillage
walkthrough.text: Pillaging is how you start battles and earn glory. Choose a province where you have at least one figure and declare a pillage. The province must still have its Pillage token. Every other player who has figures in that province is automatically drawn into the fight. Pillaging itself costs zero Rage, but you need to have spent Rage getting your figures there first.

walkthrough.title: Resolving Combat
walkthrough.text: When a pillage is declared, each player in the battle simultaneously picks one card from their hand and places it face down. Everyone reveals at the same time. Your total strength equals the strength of all your figures in that province plus your card bonus. The player with the highest total wins. If the highest strength is tied, all tied players lose, their figures go to Valhalla, and nobody gets the reward.

walkthrough.title: Battle Rewards and Losses
walkthrough.text: The winner of a pillage earns glory equal to their current Axes stat and flips the Pillage token to claim its reward, typically a stat upgrade. All losing players have their figures in that province destroyed and sent to Valhalla. Some battle cards have special effects beyond strength, like stealing glory or rewarding you for losing. Once pillaged, a province cannot be pillaged again that Age.

walkthrough.title: Understanding Your Clan Stats
walkthrough.text: Your three stats define your power level. Rage determines how many actions you get per Age. Axes determines how much glory you earn per battle won. Horns determines how many figures you can field at once. Upgrading these stats is crucial. A player with eight Rage gets far more actions than one still sitting at six. Stat upgrades come from pillage rewards and upgrade cards.

walkthrough.title: Ships and Their Special Role
walkthrough.text: Ships are placed on fjords, which are the waterways between provinces. A ship counts as being present in both adjacent provinces simultaneously. This means one ship can participate in battles in two different provinces and helps you meet the requirement of having figures present to initiate a pillage. Ships are powerful force multipliers.

walkthrough.title: Phases Three Through Six
walkthrough.text: After all players pass, the remaining phases resolve quickly. In the Discard phase, everyone discards leftover hand cards. In the Quest phase, players reveal committed quests and score any they completed. In the Ragnarok phase, the doomed province is destroyed and all figures there go to Valhalla. Finally, in the Release Valhalla phase, all figures in Valhalla return to their owners' reserves for the next Age.

walkthrough.title: Ragnarok and the Doomed Province
walkthrough.text: At the end of each Age, the province marked with the Doom token is destroyed. That province is permanently removed from the game, and any figures still there are sent to Valhalla. Before Ragnarok resolves, the next Age's Ragnarok token is revealed so you know which province is doomed next. Be sure to move your figures out unless you are deliberately using a Loki strategy.

walkthrough.title: Game End and Scoring
walkthrough.text: The game ends after Age three's Ragnarok phase. There is no separate end-game scoring. Your glory track position throughout the game is your final score. The player with the most glory wins. If tied, the player with the most remaining figures wins. If still tied, the player with the most upgrade cards on their clan sheet wins.

walkthrough.title: Welcome to the Tutorial
walkthrough.text: In this practice tutorial, we will walk through a scripted Age one with four example players, each making different choices so you can see how the game works. Follow along even if you have fewer players at your table. Ready to raid? Let us go.

walkthrough.title: The Gods' Gifts Draft
walkthrough.text: Age one begins with the card draft. Each player receives eight cards. Look through your hand and pick one card to keep, then pass the rest clockwise. You will do this six times, keeping six cards total. Try to draft cards that work together toward a single strategy rather than grabbing the strongest card in isolation.

walkthrough.title: Player One Drafts with a Plan
walkthrough.text: Player one notices a strong Warrior upgrade and a matching battle card in the opening hand. They pick the Warrior upgrade first, planning to build a combat-focused strategy. Each subsequent pick reinforces this plan with battle cards and a quest that rewards winning pillages. This is how good drafting works. Every card supports the same goal.

walkthrough.title: Player Two's Mistake: Drafting Without Focus
walkthrough.text: Player two grabs whatever looks strongest from each hand. They end up with two battle cards, one quest, two unrelated upgrades, and a monster card. Nothing synergizes. Their battle cards are mediocre because they passed the good ones while chasing flashy upgrades. They have a little bit of everything but nothing that works together.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Draft a Coherent Strategy
walkthrough.text: The draft is the most important phase of every Age. Decide on a direction with your first two picks and commit to it. A focused hand of six cards that synergize will outperform six individually strong cards every time. If you see Loki cards, go all in on Loki. If you see combat cards, build for pillaging.

walkthrough.title: Starting the Action Phase
walkthrough.text: Everyone has six Rage to spend this Age. Player one goes first. They play a Warrior upgrade from their hand onto their clan sheet for zero Rage. This gives their Warriors bonus strength permanently. Smart move because upgrades are free and power up everything you do afterward.

walkthrough.title: Player Two Invades
walkthrough.text: Then on a later turn, player two invades again into the same province. Two figures, two Rage spent. They now have some board presence, but they have not set up any upgrades or committed any quests yet.

walkthrough.title: Player Two's Mistake: All Rage on Invasions
walkthrough.text: Player two keeps invading, spending one Rage per Warrior. After four invasions they have used four of their six Rage just placing figures on the board. The problem is that figures sitting on the board do not score glory. You need to pillage to earn points, and player two barely has enough Rage left to do anything else.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Invade with Purpose
walkthrough.text: Every invasion should serve a specific goal. Place figures where you intend to pillage, not just to fill the board. Your Leader invades for free, so always deploy your Leader first. Budget your Rage so you have enough left for marching and initiating pillages after your figures are deployed.

walkthrough.title: Player Three Commits a Quest
walkthrough.text: Player three places a Quest card face down beside their clan sheet for zero Rage. The quest will reward glory if they have figures in specific provinces at the end of the Age. Now player three invades into those target provinces. Every action supports the quest goal. This is efficient play.

walkthrough.title: Player Four Starts the Loki Path
walkthrough.text: Player four drafted Loki-themed cards during the Gods' Gifts phase. They play an upgrade that grants glory whenever their figures are destroyed. Then they invade into the Ragnarok province on purpose. Other players think player four made a mistake, but player four wants those figures destroyed.

walkthrough.title: Player One Pillages
walkthrough.text: Player one has their upgraded Warriors in position. They declare a pillage in a province where player two also has a figure. Both players select a battle card face down and reveal simultaneously. Player one's upgraded Warriors plus a strong battle card give them the highest strength. Player one wins, earns glory equal to their Axes stat, and claims the pillage reward.

walkthrough.title: Player Three's Mistake: Attacking the Loki Player
walkthrough.text: Player three sees player four's lone figure in a province and thinks it is an easy win. They march in and initiate a pillage. Player four plays a Loki battle card that grants glory for losing. Player three wins the battle but player four earns just as much glory from the loss. Even worse, player four's destroyed figure triggers their upgrade that rewards destruction.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Read the Table Before Attacking
walkthrough.text: Always check what upgrades your opponents have played before picking a fight. If someone has Loki cards visible on their clan sheet, attacking them may give them exactly what they want. Sometimes the best battle is the one you do not fight. Let the Loki player's figures die to Ragnarok instead of feeding them glory through combat.

walkthrough.title: Player Two's Mistake: Ignoring Quests
walkthrough.text: The Age is winding down and player two has not committed a single quest. They spent all their time invading and fighting. Meanwhile player three scores a completed quest worth eight glory during the Quest phase. That is nearly three battles worth of glory earned for free. Player two left those points on the table entirely.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Always Commit Quests
walkthrough.text: Quests cost zero Rage to commit and offer some of the biggest glory payoffs in the game. Even if you are not sure you will complete one, there is no penalty for failing. Draft at least one quest per Age and commit it early so you can plan your actions around fulfilling it.

walkthrough.title: The Ragnarok Phase
walkthrough.text: All players have passed and the remaining phases resolve. Cards are discarded, quests are scored, and then Ragnarok strikes. The doomed province is destroyed. Player four had figures there on purpose and earns glory from their Loki upgrade as those figures head to Valhalla. Everyone else moved their figures out in time.

walkthrough.title: Release Valhalla and Reset
walkthrough.text: All figures in Valhalla return to their owners' reserves. Your Rage refills to your current Rage stat value. The next Ragnarok token is revealed. You are ready to draft Age two cards, which are noticeably more powerful than Age one. The cycle begins again with Gods' Gifts.

walkthrough.title: What Player Two Should Do Differently
walkthrough.text: Looking back, player two's biggest problems were spending all their Rage on invasions without pillaging, drafting cards without a coherent strategy, and completely ignoring quests. In Age two, player two should upgrade first, deploy with purpose, commit a quest early, and save enough Rage for at least one pillage attempt.

walkthrough.title: Continue Playing
walkthrough.text: Play continues through Ages two and three following the same six phases. Remember that Age three cards are dramatically more powerful, so position yourself in the early Ages to capitalize on the explosive finale. The board also gets smaller as Ragnarok destroys one province per Age, forcing tighter conflicts.

walkthrough.title: Congratulations
walkthrough.text: You have seen how the draft shapes your strategy, how Rage management determines your tempo, and how different approaches like combat dominance and Loki strategies can both succeed. Remember to draft with focus, invade with purpose, and never skip your quests.

walkthrough.title: Before You Rage
walkthrough.text:
Before you dive into a full game, here are three things that can help.

First, check out General Tips for strategy basics and common mistakes to avoid.

Second, reference the Appendix for a quick guide to actions, card types, and game terms.

And most importantly, ask your personal GM Guide for help anytime. Just tap the Q&A tab and ask any question during your game. Have fun!

walkthrough.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
walkthrough.text: Before we talk strategy, here are the biggest traps new players fall into. First, spending all your Rage on invasions without pillaging. Figures on the board score nothing by themselves. Second, ignoring quests entirely, which leaves huge glory rewards unclaimed. Third, drafting without a plan and ending up with cards that do not work together. Fourth, attacking a player running a Loki strategy, which gives them exactly what they want. Keep these in mind and you will avoid the most painful beginner errors.

walkthrough.title: Rage Management is Everything
walkthrough.text: Every point of Rage is one action. Players who manage Rage efficiently get more done each Age than their opponents. Always deploy your Leader first since it costs zero Rage. Play your free upgrades and commit quests before spending Rage on invasions. Budget so you always have enough Rage left for at least one march and one pillage. A player who upgrades their Rage stat early gets a compounding advantage across all three Ages.

walkthrough.title: Upgrade Stats Aggressively
walkthrough.text: Stat upgrades are the engine of Blood Rage. A player who raises their Rage to eight in Age one gets two extra actions per Age for the rest of the game. Higher Axes means each battle won is worth more glory. More Horns means you can field a larger army. Prioritize stat upgrades in Age one, especially Rage and Axes. The returns compound across all three Ages, so early investment pays off enormously.

walkthrough.title: Draft to a Plan Every Age
walkthrough.text: The Gods' Gifts draft is where games are won and lost. Make your first two picks count and let them define your direction for the Age. The four main archetypes are combat dominance, quest focus, Loki destruction, and monster rush. You do not need to pick the same archetype every Age, but within a single Age your six cards should tell one coherent story. Pass cards that do not fit your plan even if they look individually strong.

walkthrough.title: Pillage Strategically
walkthrough.text: Do not initiate a pillage unless you are confident you can win, or unless losing serves your Loki strategy. Failed pillages waste your positioning and send your figures to Valhalla. Check the reward on the Pillage token before committing. Some provinces offer stat upgrades worth fighting hard for, while others are less valuable. Also consider who else has figures in the province and estimate their likely strength before committing your best battle card.

walkthrough.title: Watch the Board and Read Opponents
walkthrough.text: Pay attention to what cards your opponents draft and what upgrades they play on their clan sheets. If someone has Loki upgrades visible, do not attack them. If someone has maxed their Axes stat, avoid provinces where they have forces unless you are certain you can win. Watch where players position their figures to predict which provinces they plan to pillage. Information is free. Use it.

walkthrough.title: Use Ships Effectively
walkthrough.text: Ships are one of the most underused tools in a new player's arsenal. A ship on a fjord counts as present in both adjacent provinces. This means one ship can participate in two different battles, help meet the requirement to initiate pillages, and force opponents to account for your strength in multiple areas. Always consider whether a ship placement gives you more flexibility than placing another Warrior.

walkthrough.title: Age Three is the Grand Finale
walkthrough.text: Age three cards are dramatically more powerful than Ages one and two. The glory rewards are bigger, the battle cards hit harder, and the quests pay out more. Use Ages one and two to build your stat engine and position yourself for an explosive final Age. Do not overspend on low-value battles in Age one. The real glory comes at the end, and the player who arrives at Age three with the best stats and the smartest draft will usually win.

summary.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
bullets: Don't spend all Rage invading without pillaging — figures don't score alone
Don't ignore quests — massive free glory left on the table
Don't draft without a plan — unfocused hands underperform
Don't attack a Loki player — you're feeding them glory

Prioritize Rage and Axes upgrades in Age 1

Can participate in 2 different battles
Helps meet pillage initiation requirements
Often more flexible than placing another Warrior

summary.title: Age Three is the Grand Finale
bullets: Age 3 cards are dramatically more powerful
Build your stat engine in Ages 1-2 to capitalize
Don't overspend on low-value Age 1 battles
Best stats + smartest draft at Age 3 usually wins

walkthrough.title: The Combat Dominance Archetype
walkthrough.text: The combat dominance strategy aims to win every pillage through raw strength. During the draft, prioritize Warrior upgrades that boost strength and high-value battle cards. Upgrade your Axes stat as fast as possible so each victory pays out maximum glory. The key is overwhelming force. When opponents see your upgraded Warriors backed by strong battle cards, many will avoid your provinces entirely, giving you uncontested pillages.

walkthrough.title: Building Combat Supremacy
walkthrough.text: In Age one, focus on Warrior strength upgrades and Axes stat boosts. Deploy your upgraded Warriors and Leader into provinces with valuable pillage rewards. By Age two, your Axes should be high enough that each battle win is worth five to seven glory. Save your strongest battle card for the most contested pillage of each Age. In Age three, your combat advantage compounds. Opponents drafted for other strategies simply cannot match your raw strength.

walkthrough.title: The Loki Strategy
walkthrough.text: This flips the game on its head. Instead of trying to win battles, you want to lose them. Draft upgrade cards that grant glory when your figures are destroyed and battle cards that reward you for losing. Commit quests that score based on figures in Valhalla. This strategy is not a consolation prize for bad players. A well-executed Loki plan is one of the most powerful approaches in Blood Rage.

walkthrough.title: Executing the Loki Plan
walkthrough.text: The trick to Loki is making opponents attack you. Place your figures provocatively in provinces others want to pillage. When they initiate, play your Loki battle card that rewards losing. Your figures go to Valhalla, triggering your destruction upgrades for bonus glory. Meanwhile, you have spent very little Rage because you let others do the attacking. The hardest part is when opponents catch on and stop fighting you. That is when you invade into the Ragnarok province for guaranteed destruction.

walkthrough.title: The Quest-Heavy Strategy
walkthrough.text: Quests offer enormous glory payoffs with zero Rage cost to commit. A quest-focused strategy drafts multiple quest cards per Age and plans all actions around fulfilling them. The key advantage is efficiency. While combat-focused players spend Rage fighting each other, you quietly position figures to satisfy quest conditions. Some Age three quests are worth ten or more glory, which rivals winning three battles.

walkthrough.title: The Monster Rush
walkthrough.text: Monsters are powerful figures with high strength values and unique abilities. The monster rush strategy recruits monsters early through upgrade cards and uses their superior strength to dominate pillages. The Troll, Sea Serpent, Fire Giant, and Mystic each bring something different. Pair monster recruitment with Horns upgrades so you can field your monsters alongside your regular forces without exceeding your figure limit.

walkthrough.title: Adapting Across Ages
walkthrough.text: The best Blood Rage players do not lock into one archetype for the entire game. They read the draft and adapt. If Age one gives you great combat cards, play aggressively. If Age two presents Loki opportunities because opponents are not drafting them, pivot to destruction. The key is recognizing what the draft is offering each Age and being flexible enough to take advantage. Your permanent upgrades from previous Ages carry forward, so build a foundation that supports multiple approaches.

walkthrough.title: Hate Drafting and Denial
walkthrough.text: Sometimes the most powerful play in the draft is taking a card you do not need to prevent an opponent from getting it. If you see a critical Loki upgrade passing around the table and you know the player to your left is building a Loki strategy, denying them that card can be worth more than any card you would pick for yourself. This is called hate drafting. Use it sparingly but decisively when you can identify an opponent's plan and disrupt it with a single pick.

summary.title: The Combat Dominance Archetype
bullets: Draft Warrior upgrades + high-value battle cards
Upgrade Axes ASAP for max glory per win
Overwhelming force discourages opposition → uncontested pillages
Strongest in Age 3 when stats are maxed

summary.title: Building Combat Supremacy
bullets: Age 1: Warrior STR upgrades + Axes stat boosts
Age 2: Axes should yield 5-7 glory per win
Save strongest battle card for most valuable pillage
Age 3: combat advantage compounds vs other archetypes

summary.title: The Loki Strategy
bullets: Draft upgrades that grant glory when figures destroyed
Battle cards reward losing, not winning
Quests score based on Valhalla count
Not a consolation prize — one of the strongest strategies

summary.title: Executing the Loki Plan
bullets: Place figures provocatively to invite attacks
Play Loki battle cards → glory for losing
Destruction upgrades trigger bonus glory on top
If opponents stop attacking → invade Ragnarok province for free destruction

summary.title: The Quest-Heavy Strategy
bullets: Draft multiple quests per Age → plan actions around fulfillment
0 Rage to commit, no penalty for failure
Age 3 quests can be worth 10+ glory each
Efficient: score glory while others fight

summary.title: The Monster Rush
bullets: Recruit monsters early via upgrade cards
High STR values dominate pillages
Pair with Horns upgrades to field monsters + regular forces
Each monster has a unique ability to exploit

summary.title: Adapting Across Ages
bullets: Read each draft and adapt — don't lock into one archetype
Permanent upgrades carry forward, supporting multiple approaches
Age 1 combat → Age 2 Loki pivot is viable if draft supports it
Flexibility beats rigidity

summary.title: Hate Drafting and Denial
bullets: Take cards you don't need to deny opponents key pieces
Most impactful against Loki — denying key upgrades cripples the strategy
Use sparingly but decisively when you can identify an opponent's plan
One denied card can be worth more than one picked for yourself

entries.definition: Your action point currency for each Age. Every action with a Rage cost depletes your supply. When you hit zero, you must pass for the rest of the Action Phase. Rage resets to your current Rage track value at the start of each Age.

Victory points in Blood Rage. Earned from winning battles, completing quests, pillage rewards, and card effects. Tracked on the glory track around the board. Your final glory total determines the winner.

The holding area for destroyed figures. When your Vikings are killed in battle or caught in Ragnarok, they go to Valhalla. All figures in Valhalla return to their owners' reserves during the Release Valhalla phase at the end of each Age.

The destruction of a province at the end of each Age. The doomed province is permanently removed from the game, and all figures there are sent to Valhalla. One province is destroyed per Age, shrinking the board over the course of the game.

The central area of the game board, representing the World Tree. Yggdrasil is a special region that cannot be pillaged and is not affected by Ragnarok. Figures can be placed there but serve a limited strategic role compared to outer provinces.

One of the eight outer regions on the board where figures are placed and battles occur. Each province has village spaces for figures and one Pillage token. Provinces are destroyed one per Age by Ragnarok.
A waterway between two adjacent provinces where Ships are placed. A ship on a fjord counts as present in both adjacent provinces for pillage and battle purposes.
Your personal off-board holding area for figures not yet deployed. All figures start in reserve at the beginning of each Age. Figures are moved from reserve to the board via the Invade action.

The card drafting phase at the start of each Age. Eight cards are dealt to each player. Players pick one card and pass the rest clockwise, repeating until six cards are kept. The two remaining cards per hand are discarded.

The main gameplay phase where players take turns clockwise, each performing one action per turn by spending Rage. The phase continues until all players have passed, either by running out of Rage or choosing to stop.
After the Action Phase ends, all players discard any remaining cards in hand. Unused battle cards, upgrades, and quests are all lost.
Players reveal any committed quest cards. If the quest condition is met, the player gains the glory reward. Failed quests are discarded with no penalty.
The doomed province is destroyed and permanently removed from the game. All figures in that province are sent to Valhalla. The next Ragnarok token is revealed for the following Age.
All figures currently in Valhalla are returned to their owners' reserves. This resets your available forces for the next Age.

Place a figure from your reserve onto an empty village space in an outer province. Warriors cost one Rage, the Leader costs zero Rage, and Monsters cost Rage equal to their strength. Ships are placed on fjords. You cannot exceed your Horns limit.
Costs two Rage. Move any number of your figures from one province to one or more other provinces. Figures may split up to different destinations. Ships march along fjords.
Costs zero Rage. Play an Upgrade card from your hand onto your clan sheet to gain its permanent ability. Includes Warrior upgrades, Leader upgrades, Ship upgrades, and Monster recruitment.
Costs zero Rage. Commit a Quest card from your hand face-down beside your clan sheet. It is scored at the end of the Age during the Quest Phase. Failed quests are discarded with no penalty.

Costs zero Rage to initiate. Choose a province where you have at least one figure and still has its Pillage token. All opponents with figures in that province are drawn into battle. Triggers combat resolution.

Cards marked with the Axes icon, played face-down during combat and revealed simultaneously. They add strength bonuses to your total. Some battle cards have special effects like stealing glory, destroying specific figures, or rewarding you for losing.
Cards marked with the Horns icon. Played onto your clan sheet for zero Rage, granting permanent abilities for the rest of the game. Types include Warrior, Leader, Ship, and Monster upgrades.

Cards marked with the Helmet icon. Committed face-down for zero Rage and scored at the end of the Age. Quest conditions include having figures in specific provinces, having figures in Valhalla, or controlling certain areas. Failed quests have no penalty.

A subcategory of Upgrade cards. When played, take the matching Monster miniature and add it to your clan as a recruitable figure. Monsters have high strength values and unique abilities. They count toward your Horns limit like any other figure.

Determines how many action points you have each Age. Starting value is six. Upgraded through pillage rewards and upgrade cards. Higher Rage means more actions per Age, which compounds across all three Ages.

Determines how much glory you earn each time you win a battle. Starting value is three. Higher Axes means bigger payoffs per victory. Prioritize upgrading this alongside Rage for maximum glory output.

Determines the maximum number of figures you can have on the board at once. Starting value is four. Includes Warriors, Leader, Ship, and Monsters. Upgrade Horns to field larger armies and contest more provinces simultaneously.

Your clan's unique hero miniature. Invades for zero Rage, making it the most efficient figure to deploy. Each player has exactly one Leader. The Leader counts toward your Horns limit like any other figure.
Basic clan figures. Each player has eight Warriors. They cost one Rage each to invade. Warriors have a base strength that can be increased with Warrior upgrade cards.
A special figure placed on fjords between provinces. Each player has one Ship. It costs one Rage to invade and counts as present in both adjacent provinces for battle and pillage purposes.

Powerful figures recruited through Monster upgrade cards. Four available: Troll, Sea Serpent, Fire Giant, and Mystic. Each has a unique strength value and ability. Monsters cost Rage equal to their strength to invade and count toward the Horns limit.

Your personal player board showing the three stat tracks for Rage, Axes, and Horns. Also has slots for placing upgrade cards. Stats start at six, three, and four respectively and are upgraded throughout the game.

Placed face-down on each outer province at setup. When a province is successfully pillaged, the token is flipped to reveal its reward, typically a stat upgrade. Each province can only be pillaged once per Age.
Placed on the province that will be destroyed during the current Age's Ragnarok phase. Serves as a warning marker so players know which province is doomed.

In combat, your total strength equals the sum of all your figures' strength values in the contested province plus the bonus from your played battle card. The player with the highest total wins. Tied highest strength means all tied players lose.

If two or more players tie for the highest strength in a battle, all tied players lose. Their figures are sent to Valhalla and no one receives the pillage reward. Avoid ties unless you are running a Loki strategy that benefits from destruction.

A deliberate strategy built around gaining glory from having figures destroyed and losing battles. Uses specific upgrade cards, battle cards, and quests that reward destruction. A competitive archetype, not a consolation prize for losing players.

When you run out of Rage or choose to stop, you pass for the rest of the Action Phase. Once passed, you cannot take any more actions this Age. Strategic passing can conserve cards and prevent overextension.
